steps.title: Set up the quadratic formula with coefficients
steps.content: The equation $w^2 + 12w - 40 = 0$ is in standard form with $a = 1$, $b = 12$, and $c = -40$. Apply the quadratic formula.

steps.title: Compute the solutions
steps.content:
Compute the discriminant: $b^2 - 4ac = 12^2 - 4(1)(-40) = 144 + 160 = 304$. Since $304 = 16 \cdot 19$, we have $\sqrt{304} = 4\sqrt{19}$.
Substitute into the formula:
$$w = \frac{-12 \pm 4\sqrt{19}}{2} = -6 \pm 2\sqrt{19}$$
The two solutions are $-6 + 2\sqrt{19}$ and $-6 - 2\sqrt{19}$. The solution $-6 + 2\sqrt{19}$ matches choice D — D is correct.
